channel.title: Cadence 步频 — PT Research Signal
channel.description: Daily curated physical therapy & rehab research for clinicians. AI-scored signal from 50 sources.

item.title: A Physical Activity Clinical Practice Guideline for People With Moderate to Severe Traumatic Brain Injury
item.description: APTA has released a new Clinical Practice Guideline for Physical Activity in People with Moderate to Severe Traumatic Brain Injury.
item.author: APTA

item.title: The effectiveness of cardiovascular rehabilitation for transient ischaemic attack and mild stroke: A randomised controlled trial.
item.description: A randomized controlled trial of 140 TIA and mild stroke participants found no significant difference in functional exercise capacity, physical activity, anthropometrics, blood pressure, or quality-of-life over 6 months between integrated cardiovascular rehabilitation and usual care.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Prevalence of eating disorders and disordered eating in athletes: a systematic review and meta-analysis
item.description: A systematic review and meta-analysis estimated the prevalence of eating disorders and disordered eating among athletes across training levels and assessment methods, identifying associated moderators.
item.author: BJSM

item.title: Patient Engagement, Clinical Outcomes, and Satisfaction of an Advanced Orthopedic Virtual Physical Therapy Model: A Retrospective Cohort Study.
item.description: A retrospective cohort study of 137 patients found that an advanced orthopedic virtual physical therapy model exceeded national completion benchmarks (70.9% vs. 43%), with 96% of completers achieving minimal clinically important differences.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Early Weight-Bearing Speeds Recovery After Unstable Ankle Fracture Surgery: study
item.description: A randomized controlled trial found that early weight-bearing and mobilization significantly speed recovery, improve functional outcomes, and progress rehabilitation after surgical fixation of unstable ankle fractures, without increasing complication rates.
item.author: Medical Dialogues

item.title: Pelvic floor muscle training for pelvic organ prolapse: A systematic review and meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials
item.description: This systematic review and meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials confirms the efficacy and safety of pelvic floor muscle training for pelvic organ prolapse.
item.author: Sports Medicine

item.title: APTA Report Finds Demand for Pelvic Health Physical Therapy Outpaces Access
item.description: A new APTA report, "APTA State of Pelvic Health Physical Therapy," reveals that demand for pelvic health physical therapy in the U.S. currently outpaces access, identifying key challenges in workforce, awareness, and timely care.
item.author: APTA

item.title: Effectiveness of non-pharmacological interventions for lower limb motor impairment in post-stroke hemiplegia: a systematic review and Bayesian network meta-analysis | Journal of NeuroEngineering and Rehabilitation
item.description: This systematic review and Bayesian network meta-analysis evaluated the effectiveness of non-pharmacological interventions for lower limb motor impairment in post-stroke hemiplegia.
item.author: Sports Medicine

item.title: Subtype-specific optimal cut-off values of the Berg Balance Scale for predicting independent walking in inpatient stroke rehabilitation: a multicentre cohort study.
item.description: A multicenter retrospective cohort study of 565 stroke patients in South Korea derived subtype-specific optimal cut-off values for the Berg Balance Scale to predict independent walking at discharge.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: The psychosocial impacts of having eating and drinking difficulties after stroke: a qualitative evidence synthesis.
item.description: A systematic qualitative evidence synthesis of 22 studies (645 informants) identified two overarching domains of psychosocial impacts—psychological and social—related to eating and drinking difficulties after stroke.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Sarcopenia Increases Adjacent Segment Degeneration Risk within 3 Years of Anterior Cervical Discectomy and Fusion.
item.description: An exploratory retrospective cohort study of 333 adult patients undergoing elective ACDF found that sarcopenia increases the risk of radiographic adjacent segment degeneration within 3 years of index surgery.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Multi-Domain Benefits of Exergaming for Sarcopenia Management in Institutionalized Older Adults: Physical, Psychological, and Social Outcomes.
item.description: A pragmatic quasi-experimental study evaluated the effectiveness of a 12-week exergaming intervention (Nintendo Switch Ring Fit Adventure) on physical, psychological, and social outcomes in 72 institutionalized older adults with sarcopenia in Taiwan.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Incidence of injury and illness at the Paris 2024 Paralympic Games: post-pandemic trends and sport-specific risks
item.description: A prospective observational study monitored 4450 athletes during the 14-day Paris 2024 Paralympic Games, reporting 535 injuries (prevalence = 10.9%) with an incidence of 8.6 per 1000 athlete days.
item.author: BJSM

item.title: GaitEncoder: A Foundation Model of Gait Kinematics for Diverse Clinical Applications and Pathologies
item.description: GaitEncoder is presented as a generative foundation model of walking kinematics that enables various downstream clinical tasks across diverse patient populations using clinically accessible smartphone video-based gait analysis, aggregating eight gait datasets from 65 subjects.
item.author: medRxiv

item.title: Prevention strategies and modifiable risk factors for upper extremity injury: a systematic review and meta-analysis for the female, woman and girl Athlete Injury pRevention (FAIR) consensus
item.description: This systematic review and meta-analysis identifies prevention strategies and modifiable risk factors for upper extremity injury in female, woman, and girl athletes as part of the FAIR consensus.
item.author: BJSM

item.title: The Impact of a Single Bout of Antagonist Stretching on Agonist Muscle Performance and Range of Motion: A Systematic review and Meta-analysis
item.description: This systematic review and meta-analysis investigated the impact of a single bout of antagonist stretching on agonist muscle performance and range of motion, addressing conflicting findings in current literature.
item.author: Sports Medicine

item.title: Exploration of lifestyle interventions for individuals with knee osteoarthritis: A scoping review.
item.description: A scoping review identified 36 studies on lifestyle interventions for knee osteoarthritis, including exercise and weight management, mapping their range, characteristics, and outcome measures.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Prevalence, Patterns, Treatment Outcomes and Costs of Physiotherapy-Managed Musculoskeletal Disorders in Nigeria.
item.description: A 10-year retrospective study of 1582 patients at a Nigerian university teaching hospital assessed the prevalence, patterns, treatment effectiveness, and costs of physiotherapy for musculoskeletal disorders.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Physical therapy protocols for patients with a megaprosthesis after bone tumor resection.
item.description: Evidence-based physical therapy protocols were developed for six types of megaprostheses after bone tumor resection through literature search and a consensus procedure involving orthopedic oncologic surgeons and physical therapists.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: In safe hands - patients with knee and hip osteoarthritis expectations and experience of physical therapist-led triage in a secondary care setting.
item.description: An explorative qualitative study using in-depth interviews with 15 patients explored expectations and experiences of physical therapist-led triage for hip or knee osteoarthritis in a Swedish orthopedic clinic.
item.author: PubMed
